title: Small Square Modern Farmhouse Table
summary: A square-topped take on the popular modern farmhouse table, built from 1x6 tabletop boards, 2x4 aprons and legs, and 2x2 under-supports.
description:
The build order here solves a real fitting problem: the 2x2 under-supports are measured and cut AFTER the tabletop boards are laid out, not before, because the actual assembled width of six 1x6 boards edge to edge varies slightly from a purely calculated number. Cutting the supports to match the real width avoids a support that's a hair too long or short relative to the top it's supposed to carry.

The side aprons are deliberately sized to overhang the 2x2 supports on the underside — the plan is explicit that this is fine, not an error to true up. It's what lets the aprons sit flush with the top and ends on the visible faces, which is where flushness actually matters for how the table looks.

cutList.note: Measure the assembled tabletop width and cut to match, rather than trusting this number exactly.

steps.title: Build and support the tabletop
steps.body: Lay the tabletop boards on a flat surface, edge to edge, and measure the actual assembled width. Cut the under-tabletop supports to match this real measurement. Glue and screw the supports to the underside with the shorter screws, keeping outside edges flush and checking for square.

steps.title: Attach the side aprons
steps.body: Measure and cut the side aprons from 2x4s. Attach so they're flush with the top and ends of the tabletop assembly — they'll overhang the 2x2 supports on the underside, which is expected. Use the longer screws.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs with the longer screws, using a square to confirm each leg sits square to the table as you attach it — two screws through the apron, then more screws driven from the inside through the 2x2 supports.

steps.title: Attach the leg-side aprons
steps.body: Glue and screw the leg-side aprons on with the longer screws to complete the base.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface smooth and apply your finish of choice.
